name: Redelay Event Source
summary: Configurable source node that subscribes to any event on the Redelay event bus. One node, every event — pick the topic via settings.
description:
# Redelay Event Source

A single configurable source node that subscribes to any event registered by any Redelay module. Replaces the old pattern of one source node per event type, which caused an N × M explosion as modules and events grew.

## How it works

The node's `eventName` setting is a JSON Schema enum whose values are injected at spec-build time by walking the live Redelay module registry. Studio renders it as a dropdown — every event declared by any loaded module appears automatically, with no manifest regeneration needed.

The output port is always the generic `EventMessage` envelope. Per-event payload shape lives in the host server's AsyncAPI document (typically at `/asyncapi.json`). In edge transforms, project payload fields via `{{.payload.*}}`.

## Use cases
- Trigger flows from any Redelay module event (`users.user_created`, `auth.login_failed`, `orders.placed`, etc.)
- Run multiple subscribers on the same event with different group IDs
- Apply server-side filters before the flow wakes (cost-effective fan-out)
- Bridge AsyncAPI-described events into FlowDSL orchestration without writing a new source node per topic

## Settings
- **eventName** — the event topic. Enum populated at runtime from the module registry.
- **groupID** — consumer group (optional). Defaults to a flow-id-scoped auto-generated value, so multiple flows subscribed to the same event each receive their own copy.
- **filter** — optional server-side filter expression, evaluated by the event bus filter chain before the flow wakes. Reduces wake-ups for high-volume topics.

## Requirements
- Host server must run the Redelay Go framework with `_ "github.com/redelay/go-events/module"` and `_ "github.com/redelay/go-events/flowdsl"` blank-imported.
- Works with any `EventBus` transport: Kafka, NATS, Redis Streams, or in-memory.

## Notes
- The `eventName` enum is a *live* view of the system — events show up the moment their module is loaded.
- For per-event payload schemas, consult the running server's AsyncAPI document at `/asyncapi.json` and reference it via `asyncapi:default#/...` in your flow.

outputs.name: Event
outputs.description: Generic EventMessage envelope emitted when a matching event arrives on the bus. Payload shape depends on the configured eventName — see the host server's AsyncAPI document.

entity_type.description: Domain entity the event relates to.

topic.description: Transport topic — matches the configured eventName.

actor.description: Who or what caused the event.

payload.description: Event-specific payload. Shape varies by eventName; consult the AsyncAPI document.

timestamp.description: When the event was published to the bus.

correlation_id.description: Optional correlation ID for tracing across events and requests.

eventName.title: Event Name
eventName.description: The event to subscribe to. This list is populated at runtime from the live Redelay module registry — every event declared by any loaded module appears here automatically.

groupID.title: Consumer Group
groupID.description: Consumer group identifier. Multiple subscribers in the same group share load; subscribers in different groups each get their own copy of every event. Leave empty for an auto-generated flow-scoped value.

filter.title: Server-side Filter
filter.description: Optional filter expression evaluated by the event bus before the flow wakes. Useful for high-volume topics where most events are irrelevant. Example: payload.total > 100 && actor.type == "user"
